_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-7 Epinephrine Auto-Injector Procedure (Revised 2/15/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Medication (All EMS Levels — MFR MCA Optional)
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-7: Epinephrine Auto-Injector Procedure — MDHHS Approved 2/15/2023
purpose: Govern administration of epinephrine via auto-injector (EpiPen or equivalent) for anaphylaxis or severe allergic reaction. Standard adult dose is 0.3 mg (>30 kg). Pediatric dose 0.15 mg for 10-30 kg. Patients <10 kg require online medical control before administration. MFR administration of epinephrine auto-injector is MCA Optional.

sections.title: Indications and Doses
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-7: Epinephrine Auto-Injector — Indications and Doses
points: Indication: anaphylaxis or severe allergic reaction with signs of respiratory distress, urticaria with systemic involvement, or hemodynamic instability
Adult dose (>30 kg): epinephrine 0.3 mg auto-injector (standard EpiPen)
Pediatric dose (10-30 kg): epinephrine 0.15 mg auto-injector (EpiPen Jr)
Patients <10 kg: contact online medical control before administration; contact MC for dosing guidance
May use patient's own prescribed auto-injector or agency-supplied auto-injector per MCA policy

sections.title: Administration Procedure
points: Preferred injection site: anterolateral thigh (mid-outer thigh) — may be administered through clothing
Place tip firmly against outer thigh at 90-degree angle
Push firmly until click is heard or felt — hold in place for 10 seconds
Remove and massage injection site briefly
Note time of administration; document in PCR
Dispose of used auto-injector in sharps container

sections.title: MFR Authorization
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-7: Epinephrine Auto-Injector — MFR Authorization
points: MFR administration of epinephrine auto-injector: MCA OPTIONAL
Only authorized if MCA has approved epinephrine auto-injector use for MFR level providers
EMT and above: epinephrine auto-injector permitted per applicable anaphylaxis protocols without additional MCA selection

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize MFR providers to administer epinephrine auto-injector for anaphylaxis
options: Authorized — MFR may administer epinephrine auto-injector per protocol criteria
Not authorized — MFR epinephrine auto-injector administration not permitted

deterioration_flags: Patient <10 kg — contact online medical control before epinephrine auto-injector administration
No improvement after auto-injector — may repeat per anaphylaxis protocol; contact online MC
scope_notes: <10 kg: contact online MC for dose guidance before administering
Site: anterolateral thigh; through clothing acceptable; hold 10 seconds
Document: time, dose, route, patient response
